qas.q: What is the mantra of the Air Force Medical Service?
qas.a: "Trusted Care, Anywhere"

qas.q: What is the AFMS Mission?
qas.a: Readiness, Better Care, Better Health, Best Value

qas.q: What are the four AFMS Focus Areas?
qas.a: Encourage healthy behaviors through a health-based culture to enhance resilience and human performance, while reducing illness and injury.

qas.q: Trusted Care
qas.a: Leadership Engagement, Culture of Safety, Continuous Process Improvement, Patient Centeredness

qas.q: What are the four domains of Trusted Care?
qas.a: Develop Trusted Care leaders at all levels in the Air Force Medical Service who are trained, experienced and equipped to enable a highly reliable organization.

qas.q: Define Leadership Engagement
qas.a: Put the health and safety of others first. Trust in leadership and in staff, willingness to admit error and identify unsafe conditions, respectful communication and the belief that safe care is everyone's duty.

qas.q: Define Culture of Safety
qas.a: See the operational environment as a system of care that can be studied to effect positive change, eliminate gaps and reduce waste. Every Airmen, Every Day, A Problem Solver.

qas.q: Define Continuous Process Improvement
qas.a: Communicate clearly and maximize value to the patient, empower patients as partners in ensuring safe care, and consistently display empathy, transparency and humility in patient interactions. Ensure patient values guide all clinical decisions.

qas.q: Define Patient Centeredness
qas.a: A management tools that helps leadership, supervisors, and trainers plan, develop, manage and conduct an effective and efficient career field training program

qas.q: What is the purpose of CFETP?
qas.a: 1-Helper, 3-Apprentice, 5-Journeyman, 7-Craftsman, 9-Superintendent

qas.q: What are the five CFETP skill levels?
qas.a: Respiratory Therapy Services

qas.q: What is the primary mission of all 4Hs?
qas.a: Spirometry, Flow volume loops, Bronchoprovocation, and Lung capacity

qas.q: What are some examples of Pulmonary function testing?
qas.a: Electrocardiograms, stress checks, Echocardiograms, Cardiac catheterization

Journeyman (5) level requires _ months of on-the-job-training.

qas.a: A minimum rank of SSgt and 12 months of UGT

qas.q: What is required to become a Craftsman (7) level?
qas.a: SMSgt

qas.a: formal indication of an individual's ability to perform a task to required standards.

qas.q: What is a certification?

qas.a: Kinked cords, cords draped over the plumbing, and worn/frayed chords or extension cords.

qas.q: What are some examples of potential electrical hazards?
qas.a: Away from structures.

qas.q: Liquid gasses are highly unstable and must be stored where?
qas.a: -297 degrees Fahrenheit and 49.7atm

qas.q: What temperature/pressure conditions must liquid gasses be stored in?
qas.a: 50psi

qas.q: What is a working pressure for liquid gasses?
qas.a: Rinse eyes, hands, and mucous membranes.

qas.q: How can you prevent burns when using corrosive chemicals?
qas.a: Alcohol, Iodophors, Glutaraldehyde, and Betadine.

qas.q: What are common chemical disinfectants in the Cardiopulmonary environment?
qas.a: Inspect for chips, cracks, and defects.

qas.q: What should you do prior to use of glassware?
qas.a: Check for patient allergies.

qas.q: What should you check for before using/administering respiratory drugs.
qas.a: Verify a physician written order for said drugs.

qas.q: What should you ALWAYS do when administering Respiratory Drugs?
qas.a: NEVER

qas.q: When can you combine two medications into one bottle?
qas.a: Right drug, right dose, right route, right time, right patient.

qas.q: Name the 5 RIGHTS of the patient
qas.a: Gloves, Mask/protective eyewear, and gowns/aprons

qas.q: What does Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) consist of?
qas.a: Provide consistent standards for expenses, manpower, performance, and reporting in military facilities.

qas.q: What is the purpose of Medical Expense and Performance Reporting (MEPRS)?
qas.a: Financial, personnel, and workload data from MTFs worldwide.

qas.q: What does MEPRS contain?
qas.a: Unit Cost

qas.q: What is the final product of MEPRS?
qas.a: Translation of medical reports into a short code used within the healthcare industry.

qas.q: What is Diagnostic Procedure Coding Medical Coding?
qas.a: International Classification of Diseases (ICD), Current Procedure Terminology (CPT), and Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System (HCPCS)

qas.q: What are the three types of code?
qas.a: The major medical fields, performance measurement, and emerging medical technology.

qas.q: What are the three categories of Current Procedure Terminology (CTP)?
qas.a: DMHRSI

qas.q: What is the only Integrated Human Resource System within the DoD?
qas.a: To manage human resources for the Defense Health Agency

qas.q: What is the purpose of DHMRSI?
qas.a: A system of moral principles that apply values to the practice of clinical medicine and scientific research.

qas.q: Define Medical Ethics
qas.a: "respect the humanity in persons"

qas.q: Medical Ethics can be summed up by a commitment to...
qas.a: Decision-making problem between two possible moral imperatives, neither of which is unambiguously acceptable or preferable.

qas.q: What is an ethical dilemma?
qas.a: Patient expectations, staffing, and quality of care.

qas.q: What are common examples of ethical dilemmas?
qas.a: Autonomy, Veracity, nonmaleficence, beneficence, confidentiality, justice, and role duty.

qas.q: What are the 7 ethical principles?
qas.a: Patients have the right to decide their own course and it is unethical to coerce a patient.

qas.q: What does autonomy mean?
qas.a: Telling the truth.

qas.q: What does Veracity mean?
qas.a: avoiding harming the patient.

qas.q: What dos nonmaleficence mean?
qas.a: Contribute to the patients health; "do no harm"

qas.q: What does beneficence mean?
qas.a: The Hippocratic Oath.

qas.q: Confidentiality is founded in what Oath?
qas.a: Formalism, Consequentialism, Virtue Ethics, and Intuitionism.

qas.q: What are the four dominant theories of medical ethics?
qas.a: Criminal and Administrative

qas.q: What are the two major divisions of legal issues facing care?
qas.a: Protects citizens or organizations from others who might seek to take unfair and unlawful advantage of them.

qas.q: What does Civil Law do?
qas.a: Civil wrong by Causing a situation that creates an opportunity for someone to be harmed, intentionally or unintentionally.

qas.q: What does Tort Law cover?
qas.a: Failure to perform duties competently. Involves the act of commission or omission.

qas.q: Define Professional Negligence
qas.a: Unreasonable lack of skill; unethical conduct.

qas.q: Define Malpractice
qas.a: Slander is verbal defamation, Libel is written defamation.

qas.q: What is the difference between Slander and Libel?
qas.a: Right and wrong described in terms of rules and principles.

qas.q: Define Formalism
qas.a: Right and wrong assessed in the amount of relative good or bad it will bring about.

qas.q: Define Consequentialism
qas.a: Ethics founded in character and justice.

qas.q: Define Virtue Ethics
qas.a: "treat others fairly"
